sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Match to sidestep Apple and Google Pay with imminent updates to Korean apps
feed.summary: Match Group plans to update its Korean dating apps by the end of October to allow alternative payment systems, bypassing Apple and Google's in-app purchase fees. This move follows a new South Korean law targeting these fees and represents an initial test of the legislation. Match Group, which expects to pay $500 million in such fees globally this year, is preparing for potential resistance from Apple and Google.

feed.title: Newmark Announces Sale of 200-Unit Multifamily Property Adjacent to Austin, Texas’ ‘Second Downtown’
feed.summary: Newmark announced the sale of Northstar Apartments, a 200-unit multifamily property in Austin, Texas, located near "The Domain" and Apple's new campus. The property was sold by Nitya Capital to Langdon Street Capital, which plans further investment in the Austin market. The sale highlights the strong growth in Austin's multifamily capital markets, with significant sales volume driven by the tech employment cluster.

feed.title: Specifications of Personal Computers Over Time
feed.summary: This article traces the evolution of personal computer specifications over 50 years, from the 1971 Kenbak-1 to modern HP Pavilion models. It highlights significant advancements in RAM and processing speed, and discusses key PC specifications like processor speed and RAM, explaining their importance and upgradeability. An infographic details spec changes for popular computers through the years.

feed.title: Jabil expects to hit over $8B in net revenue in Q1 of 2022
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. (NYSE: JBL) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year, with net revenue of $7.4 billion and $29.3 billion respectively, despite supply chain challenges. Looking ahead to Q1 2022, the company projects net revenue between $8 billion and $8.6 billion, driven by its diversified business model and opportunities in vehicle electrification, connected healthcare, and infrastructure development. Jabil continues to work with major clients such as Amazon, Tesla, Apple, and Johnson & Johnson.

feed.title: INSIGHTS | Conservative investors are pairing portfolio with principles
feed.summary: Tom Carter, a mutual fund executive, has launched the American Conservative Values ETF to allow conservative investors to align their portfolios with their principles, avoiding "woke" companies. The fund, which began in October and already manages over $28 million, aims to provide an option for individuals who wish to invest in companies that share their conservative values rather than those that engage in social and racial justice advocacy. This initiative reflects a broader movement where conservatives are using economic choices to "fight back" against companies perceived as aligning with liberal politics.

feed.title: Sonos Beam (2021, 2nd Gen) review: Compact soundbar delivers plenty of clout
feed.summary: The Sonos Beam (2nd Gen) is presented as an excellent compact soundbar that, despite sharing much of its architecture with its predecessor, notably adds virtual Dolby Atmos support, a new processor, and an updated aesthetic with a plastic grille. While it doesn't offer true Dolby Atmos like larger, pricier alternatives, its virtual surround sound experience is highly effective for its size, especially when paired with additional Sonos speakers. The soundbar also boasts a sleek design, extensive smart features including voice assistants and AirPlay 2, and numerous music streaming options, making it a versatile choice for enhancing both TV audio and general music listening.

feed.title: Online offers life-raft as local sports radio airwaves get rough
feed.summary: Following Bell Media's abrupt closure of sports talk radio stations in several Canadian cities, including Vancouver, former hosts have successfully transitioned to online platforms. Shows like "Sekeres & Price" and "Donnie and Dhali – The Team" are thriving by offering independent, digital content, becoming a new business model for sports radio and demonstrating the strong demand for local sports talk.

feed.title: Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, Disney Among Major Corporations Lying About Supporting Climate Action
feed.summary: A new analysis by watchdog group Accountable.US reveals that major corporations like Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Disney are funding lobbying groups that actively work against significant climate legislation, despite publicly claiming to support climate action. These companies are reportedly supporting or directing organizations such as the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Business Roundtable in their efforts to defeat the $3.5 trillion Build Back Better Act. This behavior is labeled as "greenwashing," where companies project an environmentally responsible image while undermining climate initiatives behind the scenes.

feed.title: Podcast Advertising
feed.summary: TEGNA offers various podcast advertising solutions, including pre-roll, episode, and series sponsorships, through its premium podcast networks: VAULT Studios and Locked On Podcast Network. VAULT Studios focuses on true crime and cold cases, with several podcasts reaching the Top 10 on Apple's charts. Locked On provides daily local sports content for major professional and college teams.

feed.title: GINN's Got the Goods of Quality Disruptive Companies
feed.summary: The article highlights the Goldman Sachs Innovate Equity ETF (GINN) as a strong investment opportunity, particularly for those seeking exposure to high-quality, disruptive growth companies. Despite the S&P 500 Technology Index's flat performance in the third quarter, GINN focuses on larger, mature yet innovative companies with high return on equity (ROE), such as Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. The fund's significant allocation to technology and healthcare sectors, combined with its emphasis on quality traits and R&D-focused companies, positions it well for continued upside.

feed.title: WSJ News Exclusive | Apple Pay Fees Vex Credit-Card Issuers
feed.summary: Credit-card issuers are growing frustrated with the fees they pay to Apple for Apple Pay transactions. Banks want Visa to alter how it processes these transactions, particularly for recurring payments. This unhappiness has increased since Apple introduced its own credit card in 2019, adding to the banks' concerns about the costs associated with the mobile wallet.

feed.title: The Beam (Gen 2) delivers in price, features and ease of use - WiFiHiFi
feed.summary: The Sonos Beam (Gen 2) soundbar offers excellent 3D sound with Dolby Atmos support at an affordable price, but fully utilizing its advanced audio features requires a newer TV with HDMI eARC and premium streaming music subscriptions. The review highlights improvements over the original Beam, such as a more powerful processor, redesigned speaker grouping, and eco-friendly packaging. It concludes that while the Beam (Gen 2) is a strong entry point into the Sonos ecosystem, the larger Sonos Arc still provides a more immersive experience.

feed.title: How DFW is getting more traction with semiconductors — and it's not just Texas Instruments
feed.summary: The Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) area is seeing increased investment and activity in its semiconductor industry, extending beyond the historical dominance of Texas Instruments. This indicates a growing and diversifying semiconductor landscape in the region.

feed.title: Ericsson sues Apple to clear proposed 5G patent licensing rates
feed.summary: Ericsson has sued Apple in a Texas federal court, seeking a declaration that its proposed rates for licensing 5G wireless patents are fair and reasonable. The Swedish telecom giant accused Apple of employing tactics to lower royalty rates and refusing to license patents under terms other than its own. This lawsuit follows a previous patent licensing dispute between the two companies in 2015, which was eventually settled.

feed.title: Ansys and Apple Develop the First Cloud-based RF Safety Testing Simulation Solution for MagSafe module MFi Developers
feed.summary: Ansys and Apple have partnered to create the first cloud-based RF safety testing simulation solution for MagSafe module MFi developers. This new Ansys solution significantly reduces costs and accelerates the certification process for Made For iPhone (MFi) MagSafe module technology by eliminating the need for physical prototypes and expensive RF safety certification software. The platform uses Ansys' HFSS electromagnetic solver, offering a streamlined, intuitive, and automated process for SAR certification, helping developers bring safe and reliable products to market more quickly.

feed.title: At a $2 Trillion Market Value, Saudi Aramco Races Apple for Most Valuable Company
feed.summary: Saudi Aramco is nearing Apple Inc.'s market valuation, reaching $2 trillion due to surging energy prices, while Apple's value stands at $2.3 trillion. This shift reflects broader economic changes, with increasing oil demand benefiting Aramco and rising bond yields impacting tech stocks like Apple. Analysts suggest oil prices could climb further, potentially pushing Aramco's valuation even higher.

feed.title: Roughly half of the Nasdaq 100 is below this critical longer-term trend line. Here's what's next
feed.summary: A recent sell-off in high-growth stocks has caused roughly half of the Nasdaq 100 stocks to trade below their 200-day moving average, a critical longer-term trend line. This comes as bond yields rise due to higher inflation expectations, making valuations of growth stocks harder to justify. While some strategists believe tech might lag as the economy reopens, others anticipate a rotation back into growth stocks after the recent decline.

feed.title: Nvidia offers EU concessions over $54 billion Arm deal
feed.summary: Nvidia has offered concessions to the EU antitrust regulator regarding its $54 billion acquisition of British chip designer Arm. The deal has raised concerns within the semiconductor industry about Arm's neutrality as a technology supplier. The EU will now seek feedback before deciding whether to approve the deal, demand more concessions, or open a formal investigation.

feed.title: Discover Equal Weight Benefits With RSP
feed.summary: This article discusses the benefits of equal weight ETFs, using the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) as a prime example. It explains how equal weighting offers diversification beyond cap-weighted funds and benefits from mean-reversion, despite critics associating its outperformance with size and value factors. The article highlights RSP's long-term outperformance potential and its role in true diversification by preventing a few mega-cap stocks from dominating the index.

feed.title: Erik Feig’s Mid-Budget Film Production Shop Picturestart Revs Up After Lockdown Pause
feed.summary: Erik Feig's production company, Picturestart, which specializes in mid-budget films, overcame early challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic. Despite launching just months before the shutdown, the company focused on development and packaging, leading to six films entering production back-to-back in early 2021. Feig emphasizes a philosophy of "discovery of voice" and a flexible financial model to support unique stories and talent.

feed.title: Paul Marshall and Ian Wace’s Stock Picks: Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL) and More
feed.summary: This article details the top stock picks of hedge fund managers Paul Marshall and Ian Wace, based on their Q2 2021 holdings. It highlights a selection of companies including Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL), Zoetis Inc. (ZTS), ViacomCBS Inc. (VIAC), and Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO), providing details on their stake value, company background, and recent analyst ratings or news. The piece also explains the investment philosophy and background of Marshall Wace LLP.

feed.title: A California investment firm went from near ruin to managing over $100 billion: Its turnaround may offer solutions to the ‘Great Resignation’
feed.summary: WCM Investment Management, a Southern California firm, rebounded from near collapse in 2011 with just $833 million in assets to managing over $100 billion by September, a tenfold increase in a decade. The firm's success is attributed to its decision to retain employees and prioritize a positive corporate culture, rather than a "toxic" one, during challenging times. This unique approach, where top management absorbed losses instead of firing staff, is highlighted as a potential lesson for companies facing the "Great Resignation."

feed.title: Chipmakers resist US bid for client data
feed.summary: Chipmakers in Taiwan and South Korea are resisting a request from the US Department of Commerce for sensitive client and supply chain data, aimed at resolving global chip shortages. TSMC has stated it will not hand over client information, while the South Korean Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy has expressed concerns about the request's scope. This resistance highlights the complexity of the issue, with Taiwanese politicians and individual investors also voicing opposition.

feed.title: TSMC and Sony considering joint chip factory, Japan gov't to help -Nikkei
feed.summary: Taiwan's TSMC and Japan's Sony Group Corp are reportedly considering a joint venture to build a chip factory in Japan, with the Japanese government prepared to contribute to the estimated 800 billion yen investment. The facility in Kumamoto is expected to produce semiconductors for automobiles and camera image sensors to address the global chip shortage, aiming for operations by 2024. Japan's Denso is also looking to participate, as the country seeks to stabilize its industrial supply chains amid concerns about chipmaking concentration in Taiwan.

feed.title: It’s Time for the Apple Watch to Break Free From the iPhone
feed.summary: This article argues that it is time for the Apple Watch to become more independent from the iPhone. It suggests Apple should allow the Apple Watch Series 7 and future models to function more autonomously. The piece also briefly mentions Apple's ambitions in car controls and an update on the Epic Games lawsuit.

feed.title: T-Mobile 5G: all the phones, deals, coverage and pricing you need
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of T-Mobile's 5G rollout in the US, detailing its different 5G technologies (Extended Range and Ultra Capacity), coverage, and performance. It also lists compatible 5G phones available through T-Mobile, discusses various service plans, and highlights T-Mobile's initiatives for business and home internet services. The article emphasizes T-Mobile's leadership in 5G availability and reliability as per independent studies.

feed.title: ClixTV Launches To Help Streamers Find Something To Watch
feed.summary: ClixTV has launched as a new streaming service designed to help viewers find content across various top subscription services, offering its own short- and long-form programming, trailers, and e-commerce opportunities. Founded by industry veterans Edward Sullivan (CEO), Stacy Jolna (CMO), and Patricia Sullivan (CCO), ClixTV aims to simplify the content search experience for consumers and provide benefits for creators, distributors, and advertisers. The platform is available on multiple devices and services including Roku, Apple TV, Amazon Fire TV, and iOS/Android.

feed.title: Understanding the Buzz Around the BUZZ ETF
feed.summary: The article discusses the VanEck Vectors Social Sentiment ETF (BUZZ), which debuted in March 2021 and capitalizes on the "meme stock" phenomenon. It examines BUZZ's methodology, noting that its longer lookback period means it doesn't solely focus on highly speculative meme stocks like GameStop and AMC. Instead, BUZZ's portfolio includes quality names like AMD, Apple, Nvidia, and Pfizer, and exhibits low single-stock risk.

feed.title: EU to investigate Nvidia's $54 bln ARM bid after remedies fall short - sources
feed.summary: The EU is expected to launch an extended antitrust investigation into Nvidia's $54 billion bid for British chip designer ARM. This comes after remedies offered by Nvidia last week failed to address competition concerns, marking a second setback for the company after a similar warning from Britain's antitrust agency. The European Commission is scheduled to end its preliminary review on October 27, initiating a four-month investigation.

feed.title: First Look at NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition Coming Exclusively to Apple Arcade on October 19
feed.summary: 2K has released a trailer for NBA 2K22 Arcade Edition, a new version of the popular basketball game optimized for Apple devices, launching exclusively on Apple Arcade on October 19. The game will feature exclusive modes like "The Association" and "MyCOURT," along with online multiplayer, Blacktop mode, and MyCAREER mode, and will include the latest 2021-22 NBA rosters. It will be playable across iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple TV for a monthly subscription of $4.99.

feed.title: Expedia CTO Rathi Murthy is helping the company simplify
feed.summary: Rathi Murthy, who took over as Chief Technology Officer at Expedia Group in May 2021, is focusing on simplifying the company's technology infrastructure. Murthy brings experience from previous roles at Gap and Verizon to her new position at the Seattle-based travel company. This move is part of the company's efforts to streamline operations.

feed.title: Apple May Cut iPhone 13 Production Goals Due to Chip Crunch
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to cut its iPhone 13 production targets for 2021 by up to 10 million units due to ongoing chip shortages impacting key suppliers like Broadcom and Texas Instruments. This reduction jeopardizes Apple's expected record-breaking holiday sales quarter, highlighting that even major tech giants are susceptible to global supply chain disruptions exacerbated by the pandemic. The shortages have already led to significant delays in iPhone 13 shipments and are affecting other Apple products as well.

feed.title: Apple to slash iPhone 13 production on chip shortages
feed.summary: Apple is expected to cut its projected iPhone 13 production targets for 2021 by up to 10 million units due to prolonged chip shortages affecting key suppliers like Broadcom Inc. and Texas Instruments Inc. This shortage is impacting Apple's ability to deliver new iPhone 13 Pro and Pro Max models, with current orders facing significant shipping delays. Despite Apple's strong buying power, the global chip shortage, exacerbated by the pandemic and an energy crisis in China, is affecting its production of other products as well, including the Apple Watch Series 7.

feed.title: How ‘Squid Game’ reveals the flaw in Netflix’s products strategy
feed.summary: Netflix's new partnership with Walmart for a digital storefront, the Netflix Hub, is criticized for its delayed inclusion of merchandise from the hugely popular show "Squid Game." While Netflix aims to diversify revenue through licensed products, the article highlights that fans are more interested in "real" items from the show, like Vans sneakers and tracksuits, which are experiencing massive sales spikes independently, revealing a flaw in Netflix's product strategy. The company's own official merchandise for "Squid Game" is described as limited and generic, not capitalizing on the immediate demand for authentic-looking items from the show.

feed.title: Qualcomm Increases Share Buyback Plan by $10B
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) has approved a new $10 billion stock repurchase program, complementing an existing plan with $0.9 billion remaining. This move led to a 1.3% increase in shares during extended trading. Wells Fargo analyst Gary Mobley maintained a Hold rating but adjusted the price target to $138.

feed.title: Apple likely to cut iPhone 13 production due to chip crunch – Bloomberg News
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly planning to slash its iPhone 13 production target by as many as 10 million units due to the ongoing global chip shortage, according to Bloomberg News. Chip suppliers like Broadcom Inc. and Texas Instruments are struggling to meet demand, impacting Apple's ability to produce the anticipated 90 million units by year-end. Despite this, some analysts believe Apple is handling the supply crunch better than competitors and maintain healthy sales forecasts for the iPhone 13.

feed.title: TSMC announces chip plant in Japan, flags 'tight' capacity throughout 2022
feed.summary: Taiwanese chip giant TSMC announced plans to build a new factory in Japan to address long-term chip demand, particularly for older chipmaking technology experiencing severe shortages. The company also indicated that tight chip supplies would likely persist through 2022 due to surging demand driven by the COVID-19 pandemic. TSMC reported higher-than-expected profits for Q3 and raised its 2021 revenue growth forecast to about 24%, highlighting strong demand across key growth platforms.

feed.title: Zepp Health banks on new smartwatches for growth
feed.summary: Zepp Health, formerly Huami Corp, is banking on its new smartwatch releases, the Amazfit GTR 3 and GTS 3 series, to drive growth. The company has seen significant global expansion, becoming the fourth-largest smartwatch vendor by shipments in Q1. Zepp Health is investing in R&D and incorporating advanced health-tracking features into its devices to compete in the growing smartwatch market.

feed.title: Medtronic pledges R&D boost, brand refresh, amid rising Big Tech rivalry
feed.summary: Medtronic is rebranding and increasing its R&D spending by 10% to adapt to the growing influence of consumers and tech giants in healthcare. CEO Geoff Martha emphasized moving "beyond devices" by investing in AI, data analytics, and robotics, acknowledging the company needs to build its direct-to-consumer presence. This strategic shift aims to speed up market entry and address chronic diseases, following a major reorganization implemented in October of the previous year.

feed.title: Sustainability is an Integral Part of ST Value Proposition Says Jean-Louis CHAMPSEIX
feed.summary: Jean-Louis CHAMPSEIX, Group VP, Head of Corporate Sustainability at STMicroelectronics, discusses the company's long-standing commitment to sustainability since its establishment in 1987. He emphasizes that sustainability is deeply embedded in ST's business model, culture, and value proposition, driving the creation of technology for a sustainable world in a sustainable way. The article highlights ST's goals for carbon neutrality by 2027, significant reductions in energy consumption, and efforts in responsible product development, aiming for one-third of its revenue from sustainable technology products by 2027.

feed.title: Zepp Health banks on new smartwatches for growth
feed.summary: Zepp Health, formerly Huami Corp, is launching new smartwatches to capitalize on growing consumer demand for smart devices. The company, which is already a top smartwatch vendor in several global markets, is focusing on enhancing user experience, battery life, and especially advanced health-tracking features to differentiate its products in a competitive market.

feed.title: S.Korea targets Apple over new app store regulation
feed.summary: South Korea is moving to investigate Apple Inc. after the company indicated it would not change its app store policy, despite a new law requiring app developers to be able to use alternative payment systems. This law, amended in August, aims to curb the market dominance of major app store operators like Apple and Google by preventing them from mandating their own payment systems and charging commissions. Google, however, has stated its intention to comply with the new regulation.

feed.title: TSMC to Build Chip Fab in Japan
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced plans to build a semiconductor plant in Japan, with construction starting next year and production by 2024. The Japanese government will provide significant financial aid for the estimated $8.8 billion investment, which aims to produce chips using 22- and 28-nanometer processes for consumer products, sensors, IoT, and auto parts. This move comes amid a global chip shortage and aligns with efforts by the U.S., Europe, and Japan to reduce their reliance on foreign chip production.

feed.title: Tile Compatibility With Android™ and Apple® iOS Devices | Tile
feed.summary: This article discusses the compatibility of Tile trackers with both Android and Apple iOS devices. It highlights that Tile works seamlessly across both operating systems, allowing users to track their belongings regardless of their phone's brand. The article also touches upon the core functionalities of Tile, such as finding misplaced items, and emphasizes its cross-platform accessibility.

feed.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger hopes to win back Apple by outcompeting it
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger expressed his hope to regain Apple as a customer, acknowledging that Intel will need to outperform Apple to achieve this. Gelsinger stated that he doesn't blame Apple for choosing to produce its own chips over using Intel's. He emphasized the necessity of Intel's products being superior to Apple's.

feed.title: Check out Cipher Mining Equity Warrant Exp 26th Aug 2026's stock price (CIFRW) in real time
feed.summary: This article provides real-time stock information for Cipher Mining Equity Warrant Exp 26th Aug 2026 (CIFRW), including its current price, historical performance metrics, key financial stats, and recent news. It highlights the company's profile as an industrial-scale data center construction and operations firm focused on bitcoin mining and high-performance computing, along with insights from analyst calls.

feed.title: Alibaba said to be launching a new home-grown chip this week
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding is set to launch a server chip this week based on Arm Holdings technology, joining other Chinese tech giants in semiconductor development. The chip’s design process concluded mid-year and it will be minted using the 5-nanometre process, showcasing China's increasing investment in chip design due to lower capital requirements. Alibaba's previous chip endeavors include Hanguang 800 and XuanTie 910.

feed.title: Apple unveils game-changing MacBook Pro
feed.summary: Apple has unveiled a completely reimagined MacBook Pro powered by the new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, available in 14- and 16-inch models. These new laptops offer extraordinary performance, unprecedented battery life, and advanced features like a Liquid Retina XDR display, improved camera and audio systems, and extensive connectivity, marking a significant leap for professional users. The MacBooks are designed for various professionals, offer remarkable power efficiency, and feature an environmentally conscious design with recycled materials.

feed.title: Apple launches faster chips, MacBook Pro laptops, Airpods at October event
feed.summary: Apple Inc. unveiled new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, updated MacBook Pro laptops, and third-generation AirPods at its October event. The new chips are faster and more power-efficient, leading to improved performance and extended battery life for the MacBook Pros which also received a new design and 1080p camera. The new AirPods feature spatial audio, longer battery life, and a new design, with the base model receiving a price cut.

feed.title: Megahit "Squid Game" puts focus squarely on Netflix's overseas growth
feed.summary: The success of "Squid Game" is prompting investors to focus on Netflix's international content strategy as a key driver for subscriber growth, especially in emerging markets, after a period of slowing additions. Analysts anticipate the megahit will boost subscriber numbers beyond estimates for the third quarter and impact the next three months. The article also highlights increased competition in the streaming market and Netflix's financial fundamentals.

feed.title: NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition Available Now on Apple Arcade
feed.summary: 2K has launched NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition exclusively on Apple Arcade, offering an authentic NBA 2K experience across Apple devices. The game includes various modes like The Association, MyCOURT, Quick Match, Online Multiplayer, and MyCAREER, featuring updated 2022 NBA rosters and top players. It is available for a monthly subscription with a free trial, playable on iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple TV.

feed.title: On-device Panoptic Segmentation for Camera Using Transformers
feed.summary: This article details Apple's HyperDETR, an on-device panoptic segmentation model for its Camera app. Built on Transformers, HyperDETR efficiently segments images for features like Portrait Mode and Photographic Styles without significant battery drain. The paper highlights the model's architecture, training, and compression techniques, making it suitable for resource-constrained mobile environments.

feed.title: Warren Buffett, Berkshire Hit $9 Billion Gain on Amex Stock This Year
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has seen its investment in American Express (Amex) increase by $9 billion this year, bringing its total unrealized gain on the stock to $26 billion. Berkshire owns 19% of Amex, a position it has held since 1998, having only invested $1.3 billion initially. Amex is the third-largest holding in Berkshire's US stock portfolio, following Apple and Bank of America.

feed.title: Are Cyber Actors Hiding in Your Pocket? Rooting Out Spyware on Mobile Devices
feed.summary: Mobile device security features, while beneficial for privacy, create challenges for forensic investigators trying to detect sophisticated spyware like Pegasus, which can exploit "zero-click" vulnerabilities. A new methodology developed by FTI Consulting utilizes threat-detection tools and AI to analyze anonymized network data and device logs, allowing for more efficient identification of compromises and the creation of detailed attack timelines without the need for traditional, time-consuming forensic processes. This approach helps investigators keep pace with evolving threats and provides a clearer picture of cyber attacks on mobile devices.

feed.title: A Look Inside The Apple iPhone 13 Pro
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Apple iPhone 13 Pro, model A2636, revealing its internal components, estimated build costs, and design wins. It highlights the A15 Bionic processor, image sensor specifications, and contributions from various suppliers like Qualcomm, KIOXIA, and STMicroelectronics. The report also compares the iPhone 13 Pro's cost and features to its predecessor, the iPhone 12 Pro, and a competitor, the Samsung Galaxy S21+.

feed.title: Target is adding Apple mini-shops to 3 more Minnesota stores
feed.summary: Target Corp. is expanding its partnership with Apple Inc., adding three new mini-shops within its Minnesota stores. This move more than doubles the number of Apple mini-shops already present in Target locations. This expansion is part of a broader strategy to integrate specialized retail experiences within Target stores.

feed.title: Snap tumbles, drags social media giants on Apple privacy tweak worries
feed.summary: Snap's shares plunged by nearly 20% after the company warned that Apple's new iOS privacy changes would have a prolonged negative impact on its advertising revenue, also dragging down other ad-dependent tech firms like Facebook, Google, and Twitter. Analysts cut Snap's price targets, expecting the impact to last into next year. The company cited global supply chain disruptions and labor shortages as additional factors compounding the advertising slowdown.

feed.title: Phunware (PHUN) stock closes up 471% on Trump Truth Social network rumor
feed.summary: Phunware (PHUN) stock surged 471% on an unsubstantiated rumor linking the company to Donald Trump's new Truth Social network. This followed a similar massive gain by Digital World Acquisition Corp (DWAC), which is merging with Trump Media & Technology Group. The article highlights the volatile and often irrational nature of the stock market, comparing these movements to meme stocks and other speculative investments in 2021.

feed.title: Apple’s New AirPods Are Pandemic-Proof
feed.summary: The review highlights the new Apple AirPods 3, emphasizing that their shorter stems make them "pandemic-proof" by not interfering with face mask loops, solving a common frustration. While also noting improved sound and battery life, the author positions the mask compatibility as the most significant enhancement. The article also discusses spatial audio, battery life, and sweat resistance, concluding with advice on which AirPods model to consider based on user needs.

feed.title: PayPal says it is currently not pursuing Pinterest acquisition
feed.summary: PayPal has announced it is not currently pursuing an acquisition of Pinterest, following media reports last week of a potential $45 billion deal. This news caused Pinterest's share price to drop over 12%, though analysts suggest Pinterest might still attract other bidders due to its valuation and recent share decline. The potential deal had faced skepticism from investors and analysts, who highlighted significant integration risks and PayPal's lack of experience in user engagement platforms.

feed.title: How’s 5G standalone doing in the U.S.?
feed.summary: Rogers Communications has launched Canada's first national standalone 5G core, highlighting a disparity with the U.S. where adoption has been slower. T-Mobile is currently the only major U.S. operator with a commercial nationwide standalone 5G network, while AT&T is still testing and Verizon is gradually integrating it. The benefits of standalone 5G, such as lower latency and network slicing, are primarily realized by enterprises and specific advanced devices, though wider consumer benefits are expected to develop over time.

feed.title: Bradesco Cartões burla regra da App Store com pedido de rastreamento
feed.summary: Bradesco Cartões is accused of violating Apple's App Store rules by restricting app functionalities if users deny tracking. The app requires tracking for full features, claiming it's for fraud prevention, despite Apple's guidelines prohibiting such demands. While Bradesco states its app passed Apple's verification, Apple has not yet commented on the situation.

feed.title: These 10 Stocks Make Up 87% of Warren Buffett's Portfolio
feed.summary: This article details the top 10 stock holdings in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, which collectively account for 87% of its $329.7 billion valuation. It highlights Buffett's belief that diversification isn't essential if one knows their investments well. The individual stocks, including Apple, Bank of America, and Coca-Cola, are discussed with brief explanations for their inclusion and performance.

feed.title: Intel 12th Gen Core Alder Lake Details at Intel Innovation 2021
feed.summary: Intel unveiled its 12th Gen Core Alder Lake architecture at Intel Innovation 2021, marking a significant architectural shift with a hybrid design combining performance (P-cores) and efficient (E-cores) cores. The platform also introduces support for DDR5 and PCIe Gen5, aiming to enhance multi-tasking performance and scalability across various form factors. This announcement sets the stage for a new generation of Intel processors focusing on diverse workloads and improved efficiency.

feed.title: Apple charges forward to 2030 carbon neutral goal, adding 9 gigawatts of clean power and doubling supplier commitments
feed.summary: Apple has significantly advanced its 2030 carbon neutral goal, doubling the number of suppliers committed to 100 percent clean energy to 175. This initiative is expected to bring online over 9 gigawatts of clean power and avoid more than 18 million metric tons of CO2e annually. Additionally, Apple is supporting 10 new "Power for Impact" projects globally to bring clean energy solutions to under-resourced communities and reduce its carbon footprint.

feed.title: Apple And Comcast Set Two-Way Streaming Distribution Deal
feed.summary: Comcast and Apple have announced a two-way streaming distribution deal. This agreement will bring Comcast's Xfinity Stream and Sky Go apps to Apple TV, and integrate Apple TV+ onto various Comcast platforms, including Xfinity X1, Flex, XClass smart TV, Sky Q, and Sky Glass. The deal aims to enhance content aggregation and user experience across both companies' ecosystems.

feed.title: Best Impact Investing Funds & ETFs
feed.summary: This article identifies top impact investing funds and ETFs for individuals looking to align their investments with social and environmental goals. It highlights five specific funds, including their expense ratios, dividend yields, and assets under management, and discusses the pros and cons of each. The piece also explains why investors choose environmentally friendly funds, emphasizing the balance between financial returns and positive societal change, and provides key considerations before investing in this sector.

feed.title: Target adding Apple display in Mandarin store
feed.summary: Target is enhancing its Mandarin store at 10490 San Jose Blvd. with a dedicated "enhanced Apple experience" display. The city issued a permit for the $310,000 project to create a 2,700-square-foot Apple display unit within the store, featuring new lighting and displays for various Apple products and accessories. This initiative, which debuted online and in select locations earlier in the year, aims to provide customers with an interactive experience supported by specially trained Target Tech Consultants.

feed.title: Apple and Amazon Stock Dives Set to Erase $200 Billion in Value
feed.summary: Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN) are expected to see their combined market valuation drop by over $200 billion when markets open Friday after both companies reported disappointing earnings. Apple's revenue missed expectations due to supply constraints, while Amazon's revenue forecast was below consensus and it warned of high costs impacting holiday quarter profits.

feed.title: Invesco ESG NASDAQ 100 ETF
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive guide to the Invesco ESG NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQMG), detailing its profile, price, dividend, valuation, expense ratio, holdings, and performance metrics. It includes vital information like its inception date, index tracked, assets under management, and comparisons to alternative ETFs. The report also highlights the ETF's ESG themes and scores, geographical exposure, sector allocation, and volatility analysis.

feed.title: Tesla's 'Sentry Mode' now offers live view of car to owner
feed.summary: Tesla has released a new software update for its Sentry Mode, allowing owners to remotely view their car's surroundings via a live camera feed on iOS devices. This feature, called Sentry Mode Live Camera Access, requires an active Premium Connectivity subscription for full functionality. The company emphasizes that the live camera feed is end-to-end encrypted and inaccessible by Tesla.

feed.title: Microsoft unseats Apple as most valuable company
feed.summary: Microsoft has surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable company following Apple's rare quarterly sales miss due to chip shortages and supply chain issues. Microsoft currently holds a market capitalization of approximately $2.48 trillion, slightly exceeding Apple's $2.47 trillion. This shift comes after Apple reported fourth-quarter sales below Wall Street's projections, while Microsoft's first-quarter revenue significantly beat expectations, driven by strong growth in its Intelligent Cloud segment.

feed.title: Move over Apple, Microsoft now the world’s most valuable company
feed.summary: Microsoft Corp. surpassed Apple Inc. to become the world's most valuable public company after Apple's shares fell due to a $6 billion sales hit from global supply chain issues. Microsoft's stock has surged over 45% this year driven by cloud-based services demand, while Apple, despite robust share buybacks, saw its market capitalization slip below Microsoft's. Both companies face ongoing supply chain challenges, though Microsoft anticipates a strong end to the year for its cloud business.

feed.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger’s “insane” plan to restore the company’s glory days
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger is implementing an "insane" and ambitious plan to regain the company's market leadership. This involves massive capital expenditures between $25 billion and $28 billion annually, along with $15 billion in R&D, to improve technology and capacity, especially in foundry business. The strategy aims for performance parity by 2024 and industry leadership by 2025, a bold move given Intel's recent struggles against competitors like Apple and ARM-based chips.

feed.title: Apple cuts iPad production to feed chips to iPhone 13 -Nikkei
feed.summary: Apple has reduced its iPad production by 50% for the past two months to reallocate components to the iPhone 13, according to Nikkei. This move highlights the impact of the global chip supply crunch on Apple, despite its strong purchasing power and supplier agreements. The company is prioritizing iPhone 13 production due to forecasted stronger demand as Western markets recover from the pandemic and increased demand in China.

feed.title: Apple adds suppliers to clean-energy pledge, including more chip firms
feed.summary: Apple announced that 175 of its suppliers have committed to using clean energy for their work, bringing over 9 gigawatts of clean energy to power grids, as part of its goal to achieve a carbon-neutral supply chain by 2030. This expansion includes key chip manufacturers like SK Hynix Inc and STMicroelectronics, adding to previous commitments from major suppliers like Foxconn and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Apple is also funding 10 smaller renewable energy projects in under-resourced communities globally.

feed.title: Another Apple Worker Says the Company Retaliated Against Her (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Janneke Parrish, a former Apple Maps program manager and leader of the #AppleToo activist movement, has filed a complaint with the National Labor Relations Board, alleging Apple fired her in retaliation for her activism. This escalation marks a growing conflict between Apple and some of its employees who have been vocal about workplace concerns. The situation signals a notable shift for Apple, which has historically avoided the kind of worker unrest observed at other major tech companies.

feed.title: Netflix Adds Mobile Games to Its App for Some Users
feed.summary: Netflix has begun rolling out mobile games to its app for Android users, including titles based on its popular show "Stranger Things." This move is part of the streaming company's strategy to attract new subscribers and increase engagement among current users. Games are expected to become available for Apple iOS devices in the coming months.

feed.title: The global chip industry has a colossal problem with carbon emissions
feed.summary: The global semiconductor industry, particularly major players like TSMC, Samsung, and Intel, faces a significant challenge with carbon emissions due to the energy-intensive chip manufacturing process. While these companies have pledged to reduce their environmental impact, their emissions continue to rise with expansion, especially for TSMC, which consumes a substantial amount of Taiwan's power output. Shifting to renewable energy sources and improving energy efficiency are critical steps being taken, but turning these pledges into actual practice across complex global supply chains remains a difficult task.

feed.title: Monthly Market Wrap - October 2021
feed.summary: The October 2021 market wrap highlights a month of economic observations including rising inflation, the Federal Reserve's anticipated tapering, and varied performance across domestic and international equities. It also covers positive trends in alternative investments and the increasing integration of ESG factors into investment strategies. The report details specific market indices and economic indicators, providing a comprehensive overview of the financial landscape during the month.

feed.title: Qualcomm forecast beats estimates as diversification efforts pay off
feed.summary: Qualcomm Inc. reported better-than-expected profits and revenue forecasts, driven by strong demand for chips across various sectors including phones, cars, and IoT devices. The company's diversification strategy, which involves supplying chips for Android phones replacing Huawei units and utilizing multiple foundries, has paid off amidst global supply chain challenges. Qualcomm's CEO Cristiano Amon confirmed that securing additional chip supplies earlier in the year was successful, contributing to their record guidance.

feed.title: Apple's software boss warns against draft EU policy on App Store
feed.summary: Apple's software boss, Craig Federighi, cautioned against a draft EU policy that could mandate allowing third-party software installations outside its App Store. Federighi argued that such a move, known as "sideloading," would make iOS devices vulnerable to malware and cybercriminal attacks. Critics, including Spotify, view Apple's stance as an anti-competitive tactic to maintain control and high commission fees, while Apple emphasizes user safety and security through its current App Store policies.

feed.title: PALCUS announces new mobile app, logo and brand identity
feed.summary: The Portuguese-American Leadership Council of the United States (PALCUS) is launching a new mobile app designed to be a central hub for the Portuguese-American community. The app will facilitate networking, share information about businesses and events, and provide resources for elected officials and organizations. Concurrently, PALCUS is unveiling a redesigned logo and brand identity to reflect its renewed focus on advocacy, youth leadership, and modern digital engagement after 30 years of service.

feed.title: How To Target Your Audience Without Third-Party Cookie Data
feed.summary: This article explores the impending demise of third-party cookies by late 2023 and its implications for digital advertising. It discusses how advertisers will face challenges in targeting ads and measuring performance, and outlines three potential scenarios for digital ad targeting: walled gardens, a consent economy, and identity innovation. The article also provides strategies for marketers to adapt, focusing on first-party data, direct publisher relationships, and contextual advertising.

feed.title: ‘Business needs a noble purpose and a human-centric approach — these core principles revived Best Buy’
feed.summary: Hubert Joly, former CEO of Best Buy, discusses how a human-centric approach and noble purpose revived the company, leading its share price to rise 11-fold. He emphasizes that putting people at the center and embracing all stakeholders is crucial for businesses today, especially in navigating challenges like the pandemic and climate change. Joly argues that the "Milton Friedman doctrine" of profit-first is outdated and highlights companies like Microsoft, Ralph Lauren, and Unilever as examples of successful human-centric models.

feed.title: How Intel plans to catch Samsung and TSMC and regain its dominance in the chip market
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has outlined an ambitious plan to regain dominance in the chip market by 2025, aiming to surpass competitors Samsung and TSMC. The strategy involves significant investment in new fabrication plants globally, totaling over $44 billion, and opening Intel's fabs to other companies through Intel Foundry Services. This move addresses past production missteps and aims to leverage government subsidies like the CHIPS Act to boost U.S. chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Supply chain problems crimp profit at Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway; cash sets record
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway reported that global supply chain disruptions significantly impacted its ability to generate profit in the third quarter of 2021, leading to an 18% rise in operating profit that still missed analyst forecasts. While net income fell 66% due to lower stock gains, the company's cash hoard reached a record $149.2 billion, reflecting reduced acquisitions and substantial stock buybacks. Supply chain issues also caused price increases for materials and freight, labor shortages, and reduced sales for various Berkshire businesses.

feed.title: The Future of Netflix’s Gaming Service Rests With Apple’s App Store Rules
feed.summary: Netflix's ambitious plans for its gaming service, aimed at customer retention and acquisition, face a significant hurdle: Apple's App Store rules. While Netflix's gaming offering is currently limited, particularly on Android, its expansion on iOS could be severely constrained by Apple's policies. This highlights a potential conflict between Netflix's strategic growth in gaming and Apple's control over its ecosystem.

feed.title: Apple Music expands Chinese music reservoir via Tencent deal
feed.summary: Apple Music is partnering with Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) to allow musicians participating in TME's "Music Cloud" program to distribute their works globally through Apple Music. This deal will give Apple Music users access to more Chinese artists. While details are vague, the "Music Cloud" program primarily supports independent musicians and comes amidst China's antitrust crackdowns on TME, pushing it to relinquish exclusive music rights.

feed.title: Adobe issued a DMCA request to remove Clean Flash, an open source project that continued to support Flash
feed.summary: Adobe has issued a DMCA request to shut down the "Clean Flash" open-source project, which aimed to continue supporting Adobe Flash Player worldwide after its official end-of-life on December 31, 2020. While Adobe officially stopped global distribution of Flash, it still supports the technology in China and for businesses, leading Clean Flash to provide a working and updated version independently. This move highlights ongoing debate within the developer community about the preservation of Flash content versus moving entirely to open standards like HTML5.

feed.title: Google’s parent Alphabet breaches $2 trillion in market value
authors: Katrina Lewis and Nico Grant and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., reached a $2 trillion market valuation for the first time, driven by strong growth in digital advertising and its cloud business. This puts Alphabet in an exclusive club with Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp., with analysts remaining optimistic about its future growth due to a cheaper valuation and increasing cloud adoption.

feed.title: An original Apple-1 computer sells for $400,000
feed.summary: An original Apple-1 computer, one of only 200 designed by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, was sold for $400,000 at auction. The computer, known as the "Chaffey College" Apple-1, was initially sold for $666.66 in 1976 and represents a significant piece of personal computing history. Its koa wood case is one of only six known to exist, making it a rare collector's item.

feed.title: Salesforce CEO Benioff invests in search engine startup You.com
feed.summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has invested $20 million in You.com, a startup aiming to challenge Google's dominance in search with a more customizable and privacy-focused engine. Benioff transferred ownership of the You.com domain name, which he had owned since the 1990s, to the startup. You.com, founded by former Salesforce AI scientists, plans to differentiate itself by not tracking users or selling "privacy-invasive ads," instead focusing on revenue from transactions and helping publishers generate ad revenue.

feed.title: Google-owner Alphabet breaches $2 trillion market cap, joins Apple and Microsoft
feed.summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., reached a $2 trillion market capitalization for the first time, driven by increased digital ad spending and growth in its cloud business. This achievement places Alphabet alongside Apple and Microsoft in the exclusive $2 trillion club. The company reported a record third quarter in 2021 with $65.1 billion in earnings.

feed.title: Google loses challenge against EU antitrust ruling, $2.8-bln fine
authors: Francois Aulner and Foo Yun Chee
feed.summary: Alphabet's Google unit lost an appeal against a 2.42-billion-euro ($2.8-billion) antitrust decision by the EU related to its price comparison shopping service. This ruling marks a significant win for Europe's competition chief, Margrethe Vestager, and could strengthen the EU's position in ongoing investigations into other major tech companies like Amazon, Apple, and Facebook. Google stated it would review the judgment and has already complied with the Commission's order to ensure a level playing field.

feed.title: What Does iOS 14.5 Mean for Marketers? A Deep Dive into Apple’s ATT and PCM
feed.summary: The iOS 14.5 update significantly impacts app advertisers due to Apple's App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework, which limits the use of Identifier for Advertisers (IDFA) by requiring explicit user consent for tracking. While web audiences are less affected, app-to-app tracking is heavily restricted, leading to fewer reported conversions. The article suggests strategies for marketers, such as separating Android and iOS traffic and optimizing for upper-funnel events, to navigate these changes.

feed.title: Tencent Music brings catalog of Chinese tracks to Apple Music worldwide
feed.summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) has partnered with Apple Music to make its catalog of Chinese music available worldwide through TME Music Cloud. This collaboration aims to introduce global audiences to China's unique music culture and genres, supporting the international development of Chinese musicians. TME reported strong growth in its independent musician platform and online music paying users in Q3 2021, despite a slight decline in overall mobile MAUs.

feed.title: Apple's original computer fetches $400,000 at US auction
feed.summary: An original Apple-1 computer, built by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs in 1976, was sold for $400,000 at a US auction. The rare, functioning computer with a Hawaiian koa wood case is one of only 200 made and had only two previous owners. This model is considered a "holy grail" for vintage electronics collectors, though it wasn't the highest-selling Apple-1 to date.

feed.title: 5 Charts on General Electric's Fall From Grace
feed.summary: This article details General Electric's significant decline in value over the past two decades, from once being the most valuable company globally to its current 75th position in the U.S. stock market. It attributes GE's fall to a deterioration of culture, poor capital allocation decisions, and an over-reliance on its financial unit. The article discusses GE's recent decision to split into three companies (aviation, healthcare, and energy), a move applauded by analysts and seen as a potential catalyst for closing the persistent price/value gap for the stock.

feed.title: Cinemark Welcomes Moviegoers to New Roseville Galleria Theatre on Nov. 11
feed.summary: Cinemark officially opened its new 14-screen Cinemark Roseville Galleria Mall and XD theatre in the Westfield Galleria at Roseville on November 11, 2021. This new location aims to provide an upgraded, immersive entertainment experience for the Sacramento area, featuring luxury loungers, XD auditoriums, laser projection, and expanded concession options. To celebrate the grand opening, Cinemark offered a one-month free trial of its Movie Club to the first 1,000 visitors.

feed.title: Exclusive: First Sonos headphones could use this RHA design
feed.summary: British audio brand RHA's cancelled "Atlas" headphones could provide the design blueprint for Sonos's first venture into the headphone market. Sonos recently acquired a majority stake in Origin North Ltd. (formerly RHA), suggesting they may leverage RHA's technology and designs. The Atlas headphones were sophisticated, featuring ANC, spatial audio, Hi-Res Bluetooth, and Google Assistant, with a projected price similar to Apple's AirPods Max.

feed.title: Extended Warranty Revenue Trends, 11 November 2021
feed.summary: This article analyzes extended warranty revenue trends through the financial data of four key companies: Assurant Inc., Frontdoor Inc., Lowe's Companies Inc., and Apple Inc. It suggests that 2020 and 2021 have been strong years for the product protection industry, despite initial pandemic lockdowns. The report details revenue and profit growth for these companies' respective extended warranty programs, concluding that the industry experienced no recession and is breaking records for growth and profitability.

feed.title: Alibaba's Singles Day sales grow just 8.5%, slowest pace ever
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd reported an 8.5% growth in sales during its annual Singles' Day shopping event, marking the slowest pace ever for the e-commerce giant. This slowdown is attributed to strong regulatory and supply chain headwinds affecting Chinese tech firms. Alibaba also shifted its focus from sales figures to social welfare initiatives, toning down the traditional marketing hype amid ongoing regulatory tightening.

feed.title: Moviegoers Can Now Use Bitcoin, Ether at AMC Theatres
feed.summary: AMC Theatres now accepts Bitcoin, Ether, and several other cryptocurrencies and digital payment platforms for movie tickets, aiming to attract crypto-savvy customers. This move highlights the growing acceptance of digital currencies in mainstream business, creating new opportunities for exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that offer exposure to cryptocurrency or blockchain technology. Investors can gain exposure through ETFs like Amplify Transformational Data Sharing ETF (BLOK) for blockchain or by investing in companies like AMC via the SoFi Social 50 ETF (SFYF).

feed.title: 6 Things You Didn't Know About Cisco Networking Systems, Inc.
feed.summary: This article reveals six lesser-known facts about Cisco Networking Systems, Inc., highlighting its origins, challenges, past market dominance, ventures into consumer electronics, divestitures, and its fastest-growing cybersecurity business. It aims to provide investors with a broader understanding beyond Cisco's reputation as a mature growth company focused on networking products.

feed.title: China’s property crackdown sinks economic growth to 90s levels
feed.summary: China's economy is experiencing a significant slowdown, reaching levels last seen in the 1990s, largely due to President Xi Jinping's crackdown on the real estate sector. This policy aims to reduce the economy's reliance on property and redirect investment towards high-tech manufacturing, despite economists predicting a substantial drop in GDP growth for 2022. The crackdown, coupled with China's strict "zero-COVID" policy, is impacting consumer spending and causing concern among global financial institutions.

feed.title: PayPal’s Legal Chief Louise Pentland to Depart at Year’s End (2)
feed.summary: Louise Pentland, PayPal's chief business affairs and legal officer, is set to depart the company on December 31st after nearly seven years. She will be replaced by Bimal Patel as general counsel in January. Pentland entered a separation agreement with PayPal, which includes a cash payment of over $2.5 million.

feed.title: Deal with Tencent Music brings Chinese song catalogue to Apple Music
feed.summary: Tencent Music Entertainment Group has struck a deal to bring music from Chinese labels and artists, managed through its TME Music Cloud, to Apple Music globally. This collaboration aims to promote Chinese music culture and artists internationally, offering distribution, promotion, royalty settlements, and data insights. The move highlights Tencent Music's expanding international partnerships despite facing domestic regulatory pressure regarding its market dominance.

feed.title: Qualcomm forecasts post-Apple sales growth, shares hit all-time high
feed.summary: Qualcomm Inc. expects its chip sales to Apple Inc. to significantly decrease in the coming years as Apple develops its own modem chips. However, Qualcomm anticipates robust growth in other sectors like autonomous cars, the internet of things, and virtual reality headsets, which led to its shares hitting a record high. The company projects substantial revenue increases from automotive and IoT divisions, offsetting the expected decline from Apple.

feed.title: Target Continues to Grow; Updates Holiday Guidance
feed.summary: Target reported strong third-quarter earnings, exceeding expectations with robust growth across all five core categories, leading the company to raise its fourth-quarter holiday guidance. Despite these positive results and increased inventory for the holiday season, Target's shares fell due to investor concerns about ongoing supply chain issues and inflationary pressures. CEO Brian Cornell acknowledged these challenges, emphasizing the company's agility in addressing them and its long-term investment in meeting customer demand.

feed.title: Apple announces Self Service Repair
feed.summary: Apple announced Self Service Repair, allowing customers access to genuine Apple parts, tools, and manuals for self-repairs. Initially available for iPhone 12 and iPhone 13 lineups in the US in early 2022, with plans to expand to Mac computers and other countries. This program provides an additional option for customers comfortable with DIY repairs, alongside Apple's existing network of authorized service providers.

feed.title: Exclusive first look inside Corning's new pharmaceutical vial factory
feed.summary: CNBC got an exclusive look inside Corning's new pharmaceutical vial factory in Durham, North Carolina, which has the capacity to produce 500 million glass vials annually. This facility, built with partial funding from Operation Warp Speed, uses Gorilla Glass technology for enhanced durability and storage capabilities, crucial for Covid-19 vaccines. Corning CEO Wendell Weeks sees billions of dollars in opportunity for the company's pharmaceutical business.

feed.title: Apple is one step closer to developing fully automated vehicles
feed.summary: Apple is accelerating the development of its electric car project, codenamed "Project Titan," with a renewed focus on achieving full self-driving capabilities that require no human intervention. Kevin Lynch, the new leader of the car team, is pushing for a 2025 launch for a fully autonomous vehicle, equipped with an advanced internally designed chip and a hands-off driving interior. This initiative marks a significant shift in strategy for Apple's long-running and often turbulent car project, aiming to tackle a major technical challenge within the auto industry.

feed.title: Apple’s First GaN Charger
feed.summary: Apple has introduced its first gallium nitride (GaN) charger, a 140W model for the new 16-inch MacBook Pro, marking a significant shift in power transistor technology for the company. TechInsights research confirms that GaN Systems secured the design win for this charger, highlighting the growing adoption of GaN in power electronics. This move by Apple, while not the first in the market for GaN, is expected to accelerate its integration into various applications, especially given the charger's use of the new USB Power Delivery 3.1 standard.

feed.title: Live Nation, Apple, Travis Scott Sued for $2 Billion Over Astroworld Disaster
feed.summary: Live Nation, Apple Inc., and Travis Scott are being sued for $2 billion by 282 concertgoers who claim they were injured during the Astroworld festival. The lawsuit, filed in Houston, alleges that the crowd of 50,000 people was "incited into a frenzy," leading to 10 deaths and hundreds of injuries. The complaint is an amendment to an earlier lawsuit and is the largest one filed to date regarding the tragic event.

feed.title: How the company that makes glass for your iPhone produces vials for Covid vaccines
feed.summary: Corning, known for Gorilla Glass and cookware, has rapidly expanded its pharmaceuticals business. The company opened a new factory in Durham, North Carolina, capable of producing 500 million glass vials annually for vaccines and drugs, supported by $204 million in federal funding from Operation Warp Speed. Corning also unveiled Velocity Vials, engineered to speed up manufacturing, which are already being utilized by pharmaceutical manufacturer Catalent.

feed.title: Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley Will Redesign AmEx Platinum Cards, and Other News
feed.summary: Artists Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley are set to redesign the American Express U.S. Platinum Card, with the new designs launching next month during Art Basel Miami Beach. American Express also announced a $1 million donation to the Studio Museum in Harlem, supporting artists of African descent and aligning with its DEI initiatives. Other news includes Burberry's "Imagined Landscapes" pop-up in South Korea, the Preston Bus Station receiving the World Monuments Fund/Knoll Modernism Prize, and the renaming of Staples Center to Crypto.com Arena.

feed.title: This company may overtake LG as OLED supplier for Apple iPhones
feed.summary: Chinese display manufacturer BOE is reportedly set to overtake LG as an OLED panel supplier for Apple iPhones. BOE is increasing its production, aiming to supply 50 million OLED units by 2022, potentially surpassing LG's projected 70 million units in 2023. Samsung remains Apple's largest OLED supplier, expected to provide 150 million units by 2022.

feed.title: Apple posts internal memo affirming employees' right to discuss pay
feed.summary: Apple has issued an internal memo affirming employees' right to discuss their wages, hours, and working conditions, a significant shift for the company following months of employee advocacy. This move comes after workers pushed for greater transparency regarding pay equity and faced restrictions on discussing these topics internally. The memo acknowledges rights protected under the National Labor Relations Act and is seen as a victory for Apple organizers who have filed unfair labor practice charges against the company.

feed.title: Amazon and Apple handed $225 million in Italian antitrust fines
feed.summary: Italy's antitrust authority has fined Amazon and Apple a total of more than 200 million euros ($225 million) for alleged anti-competitive cooperation. The fines stem from a 2018 agreement that restricted the sale of Apple and Beats products on Amazon.it to only selected resellers, violating EU rules and impacting price competition. Both companies deny wrongdoing and plan to appeal the decision.

feed.title: Apple sues company known for hacking iPhones on behalf of governments
feed.summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against NSO Group, an Israeli firm that develops software to hack iPhones, alleging that the company weaponizes powerful state-sponsored spyware against innocent users. Apple seeks a permanent injunction to prevent NSO Group from using its software, services, or devices and is seeking damages exceeding $75,000. Additionally, Apple will donate $10 million and any lawsuit damages to organizations combating digital surveillance, while also pledging to inform iPhone users targeted by Pegasus malware.

feed.title: Apple sues Israeli firm NSO Group over spyware
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has filed a lawsuit against Israeli firm NSO Group, accusing it of using its Pegasus spyware to hack Apple devices and enable human rights abuses. This legal action follows previous revelations about Pegasus being used against journalists and activists, and comes after WhatsApp's successful lawsuit against NSO and US sanctions against the company. Apple is seeking financial compensation and a permanent ban on NSO from using its servers and devices, pledging to contribute damages from the lawsuit to groups fighting cyber-surveillance.

feed.title: 20-Year Returns: Monster Eats Apple for Breakfast
feed.summary: Over the past 20 years, Monster Beverage Corporation's stock has outperformed major tech companies like Apple and Amazon, increasing by over 100,000 percent. The company, an early entrant in the energy drink market, has grown significantly, recording $1.4 billion in profit on $4.6 billion in sales in 2020. Monster Beverage is now a dominant player in the energy drink market and has recently been in merger talks with Constellation Brands.

feed.title: Apple files lawsuit against NSO Group, saying U.S. citizens were targets
feed.summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against Israeli cyber firm NSO Group and its parent company OSY Technologies, alleging surveillance and targeting of U.S. Apple users with its Pegasus spyware. The iPhone maker is seeking to ban NSO Group from using any Apple software, services, or devices and will donate $10 million, plus any damages, to cybersurveillance research groups. NSO maintains that its tools are sold only to governments and law enforcement and have saved "thousands of lives."

feed.title: Four Ways to Reduce Security Risks Using Software Updates and Deployment Resources
feed.summary: This article outlines four key strategies for organizations to enhance their cybersecurity posture by effectively managing software updates and deployments. It emphasizes the importance of subscribing to security notifications, promptly installing updates, utilizing enterprise deployment tools, and replacing unsupported software. The guidance aligns with CISA's directive on reducing known exploited vulnerabilities and highlights Adobe's resources and support for these initiatives.

feed.title: Smart tattoos and the future of wearable devices - Hardware Pioneers
feed.summary: The wearable technology market is experiencing significant growth, projected to reach $57,653 million globally by 2022. The article discusses various types of modern wearables, including smartwatches, smart jewelry, and smart clothing, highlighting their capabilities in health tracking and lifestyle improvement. Looking ahead, the article points to the emergence of "smart tattoos" that will integrate flexible electronic sensors into the skin to monitor vital signs and bodily functions.

feed.title: Apple warns Thai activists "state-sponsored attackers" may have targeted iPhones
feed.summary: Apple Inc. issued alert messages to at least six Thai activists and researchers, warning them that their iPhones may have been targeted by "state-sponsored attackers." This comes after Apple filed a lawsuit against Israeli cyber firm NSO Group for alleged surveillance using its Pegasus spyware. The Thai individuals targeted are perceived as critical of the current Thai government.

feed.title: Best Buy heist: Burglars cut hole in store roof, steal merchandise ahead of Black Friday
feed.summary: In Spanish Fort, Alabama, burglars cut a hole in the roof of a Best Buy store and stole a significant amount of computer and electronics equipment, primarily Apple products, just days before Black Friday. Police described the incident as a professional operation, noting the thieves spent several hours inside the store and left a ladder and tools behind. Best Buy's CEO recently acknowledged an increase in theft affecting employees and company security.

feed.title: FreeStyle Libre 2 Connected to Your Phone
feed.summary: Abbott announced that the FDA has cleared its FreeStyle Libre 2 apps for iPhone and Android users, allowing compatible smartphones to connect to FreeStyle Libre 2 sensors. This update addresses the growing demand for digital and connected diabetes care, enabling users to discreetly monitor their glucose with a simple scan. The FreeStyle Libre system, which now includes the ability to connect to iOS and Android devices, continues to evolve with advanced features and accuracy, serving millions of users worldwide.

feed.title: 'Spotify Wrapped' Recaps Your Listening History for 2021
feed.summary: Spotify has launched its annual "Spotify Wrapped" feature for 2021, allowing users to review their top artists, genres, songs, and podcasts. This year's Wrapped includes new interactive elements like "2021: The Movie," "Your Audio Aura," and "Playing Cards," and can now be shared on TikTok. While Apple Music offers a "Replay 2021" playlist, it lacks the in-depth personalized data and social sharing features that make Spotify Wrapped popular.

feed.title: Facebook parent grabs another 1M sf in Bay Area
feed.summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) has leased an additional 1 million square feet of office space in the Bay Area, specifically in Sunnyvale and Burlingame, to reduce employee commute times and support its net-zero carbon emission goal. The lease of four buildings in Sunnyvale, totaling over 700,000 square feet, is considered the largest private-sector office lease in the U.S. this year. This expansion further solidifies Sunnyvale's position as a key office market in Silicon Valley.

feed.title: Apple loses a top vehicle engineer to flying-taxi startup Archer Aviation
feed.summary: Michael Schwekutsch, a top vehicle engineer previously with Apple's "Project Titan" electric vehicle initiative, has joined Archer Aviation, a startup focused on building flying taxis. His departure comes as Apple tries to accelerate its self-driving car project, sparking discussions among analysts about Apple Car's potential impact on the broader EV market, particularly on Tesla.

feed.title: What Stocks Are in QQQ? Rebound Likely, Good Long-Term Pick
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq 100, is a tech-heavy fund that experienced a nosedive following the omicron variant news and inflation concerns in December 2021. Despite this slump, the article suggests that QQQ is likely to recover due to its diverse holdings and past performance, making it a potentially smart long-term investment. The article details the top 20 holdings and the industry breakdown of the ETF.

feed.title: Apple warns suppliers of weak demand for iPhone 13 lineup - Bloomberg
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has reportedly informed its parts suppliers that demand for the iPhone 13 lineup has slowed, indicating a decrease in consumer interest for the newest models. This comes after Apple had already cut production targets due to a global chip shortage, and now even those revised numbers appear unlikely to be met. The news led to a drop in Apple's shares and those of its suppliers, as consumers shift spending from new electronics to experiences like travel and outdoor activities post-pandemic.

feed.title: U.S. sues to block Nvidia’s $40-billion chip takeover of Arm
feed.summary: U.S. antitrust officials have sued to block Nvidia's proposed $40-billion acquisition of Arm Ltd., citing concerns that the deal would stifle innovation and undermine competition in the semiconductor industry. The Federal Trade Commission argues that the merger would grant Nvidia excessive control over chip designs widely used across various technologies. The acquisition, initially valued at $40 billion in September 2020, has faced regulatory hurdles globally due to Arm's critical role as a technology licensor to hundreds of companies including Nvidia's rivals.

feed.title: Target's gift card sale returns this weekend with 10% off discount. Here's how to save.
feed.summary: Target is bringing back its annual gift card sale this weekend, offering a 10% discount on purchases up to $500 for Target store gift cards. To access the discount, customers must be members of the free Target Circle loyalty program. The gift cards can be used for personal shopping or as gifts and are redeemable in-store or online.

feed.title: U.S. FTC sues to block Nvidia deal to buy Arm
feed.summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has sued to block Nvidia Corp's more than $80 billion acquisition of British chip technology provider Arm, citing concerns that the deal would give Nvidia undue control over crucial computing technology and designs. This move complicates an already challenging regulatory path for the acquisition, which has faced opposition from the chip industry and scrutiny from British and European Union regulators. The FTC argues the merger would harm competition, reduce innovation, and lead to higher prices by giving Nvidia the ability and incentive to undermine competitors who rely on Arm's technology.

feed.title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
feed.summary: An article discussing the importance of balancing tech investments with high-quality stocks. It highlights Mark Mahaney's "dislocated high-quality" (DHQ) investment tenet and introduces the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as a suitable option for investors seeking quality and reduced volatility.

feed.title: Can Apple Take Down the World’s Most Notorious Spyware Company?
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has filed a lawsuit against NSO Group Technologies and Q Cyber Technologies, the creators of the Pegasus spyware, which has been used to hack iPhones of journalists, human rights defenders, and government officials worldwide. The article details the widespread use and harmful impact of Pegasus, the background of previous legal actions against NSO Group, and analyzes Apple's legal strategy, highlighting its aggressive stance in protecting user security. The outcome of the lawsuit could significantly impact the future of malicious spyware and state-sponsored hacking.

feed.title: Netflix, Disney Sue Streaming Sites for Copyright Infringement
feed.summary: Netflix, Disney, and several other major entertainment companies have filed a lawsuit against two streaming sites, AllAccessTV (AATV) and Quality Restreams, alleging copyright infringement. The companies claim that these sites are illegally selling access to their copyrighted movies and television programs without permission. The lawsuit was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California and includes plaintiffs such as Universal Studios, Paramount Pictures, Amazon, Apple, and Warner Bros.

feed.title: Samsung begins One UI 4 rollout to the Galaxy Note 10 and Galaxy Note 10 Plus with first beta build
feed.summary: Samsung has initiated the One UI 4 beta rollout to the Galaxy Note 10 and Galaxy Note 10 Plus, making the new OS available in South Korea for unlocked devices and those from KT Corporation. While stable builds are anticipated in January 2022 for these models, following the Galaxy S21 series' update in November, the beta launch for the Note 10 series suggests a wider release will follow. This update brings Android 12 to the former flagship devices, with expectations for stable releases next month.

feed.title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
feed.summary: A market expert suggests investors prioritize high-quality stocks over solely focusing on big tech names. Mark Mahaney of Evercore ISI advocates for investing in "dislocated high-quality" companies with large total addressable markets and strong innovation, rather than just trading tech stocks. The article highlights the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as an example of an ETF that focuses on quality while managing volatility.

feed.title: Nasdaq dives over 2% as tech stocks slide at end of volatile week
