feed.summary: Wall Street's major indexes, particularly the Nasdaq, experienced a significant decline as tech stocks slid at the end of a volatile week. This downturn was influenced by mixed jobs data, uncertainty surrounding the Omicron coronavirus variant, and the Federal Reserve's policy tightening signals. Major tech companies such as Apple, Meta Platforms, Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla all saw their shares fall, contributing to the broader market dip.

feed.title: Nvidia metaverse future bright even as Arm may slip from grasp
feed.summary: Nvidia's acquisition of British chip technology provider Arm faces significant hurdles, with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission suing to block the deal and analysts doubting its completion. Despite this potential setback, Nvidia's future prospects remain strong, driven by its focus on artificial intelligence (AI) and the metaverse, areas where it is a leading player. The company's data center business is thriving as internet companies invest heavily in augmented and virtual reality technologies.

feed.title: The metaverse needs aggressive regulation
feed.summary: The author, a pioneer in AR/VR technology, argues for aggressive regulation of the metaverse to prevent large corporations from extensive monitoring and manipulation of users. He emphasizes the need to restrict data collection, regulate real-time marketing tactics, and ensure transparency regarding AI-controlled agents and targeted placements. The article warns that without regulation, the metaverse risks becoming an environment where powerful entities exploit user data and emotions for commercial gain.

feed.title: Market history says omicron volatility isn't a reason for investors to sell
feed.summary: Market history suggests that volatility caused by the omicron variant should not prompt investors to sell, according to Keith Lerner of Truist. He notes that 18 out of 19 major VIX spikes since 1990 were followed by S&P 500 gains a year later, averaging 20%. While short-term volatility is expected, the long-term trend, supported by an expanding economy and adapting consumers and corporations, remains positive.

feed.title: Apple iPhone 13 Rebates Fail to Deliver for Some Buyers
feed.summary: Some customers are experiencing issues with wireless carriers denying rebates offered to promote new Apple Inc. iPhones, iPads, and Watches. This problem comes years after wireless carriers eliminated deep subsidies for phones, forcing buyers to contend with the full cost of devices. The article also briefly mentions the impact of the omicron variant on Apple and another executive departure.

feed.title: Stellantis to Rely on Partners in $23 Billion Software Push
feed.summary: Stellantis announced a strategy to generate approximately $23 billion in additional revenue by 2030 through software-driven features in its vehicles. The automaker plans to leverage partnerships with companies like Foxconn, Waymo, and BMW to develop three new technology platforms by 2024. This initiative aims to significantly increase the number of vehicles capable of generating software revenue to 34 million.

feed.title: Shachar Scott Joins Meta Reality Labs as Director of Brand & International Marketing
feed.summary: Shachar Scott has been appointed as the Director of Brand and International Marketing at Meta Reality Labs, joining on December 6th, 2021. In this role, she will oversee global marketing, creative, media, and production, reporting to Brian Irving. Scott brings extensive experience from previous positions at Bumble, Snapchat, and Apple, where she focused on building and scaling global brands.

feed.title: Apple's alleged restriction on workers' Slack use sparks labor complaint
feed.summary: A former Apple employee has filed a complaint with a U.S. labor regulator, alleging that Apple has restricted employees' use of the workplace messaging app Slack. The complaint claims these restrictions hinder workers' ability to engage in protected speech and organizing. This action comes amidst increasing employee activism at Apple regarding issues like pay equity and remote work, with the former employee claiming she was fired after leading such efforts.

feed.title: How much control should Apple have over your iPhone and the App Store?
feed.summary: Apple is facing increasing scrutiny over its tight control of the App Store, which critics call a monopoly due to its exclusive app distribution, mandatory commissions, and content restrictions. While Apple argues these measures ensure user security and privacy, lawmakers and regulators worldwide are pushing for changes to increase competition, potentially leading to more app choices and lower prices for consumers, but also raising concerns about security. Despite some minor concessions, Apple largely maintains its stance, suggesting significant shifts may only occur if forced by new legislation or legal challenges.

feed.title: Apple Nears $3 Trillion in Market Value. Here’s Some Context
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is close to reaching a $3 trillion market value, needing just a 6% increase in its stock price to hit this milestone. This achievement would make it the first company to reach such a valuation, less than four years after first surpassing $1 trillion. The company's size now rivals the entire German equity market or the U.K. economy.

feed.title: B2C E-commerce Market May Set a New Epic Growth Story |
feed.summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics titled "B2C E-commerce Market Insights, to 2026" forecasts significant growth for the B2C e-commerce market, driven by increasing disposable income, global per capita income, and expanding internet penetration. Key players in this evolving market include Alibaba, Amazon, eBay, and Apple. The report details market trends, opportunities, drivers, and challenges, segmenting the market by type, application, brand, and product across various regions.

feed.title: Sonos now supports Dolby Atmos and 24-bit hi-res audio on Amazon Music
feed.summary: Sonos has expanded its audio support to include Amazon Music Unlimited's hi-res and Dolby Atmos music. This allows Amazon Music subscribers to stream "Ultra HD" (24-bit) tracks and Dolby Atmos content on compatible Sonos devices. While Amazon offers up to 24-bit/192kHz, Sonos devices will stream up to 24-bit/48kHz, with broader compatibility for hi-res than for Dolby Atmos which is limited to the Beam Gen 2 and Arc.

feed.title: Peterbilt Techs to Get Augmented Reality Tool
feed.summary: Peterbilt Motors Co. plans to deploy 200 of its ARTech augmented reality tools to dealer locations by March 31, 2022, to enhance customer uptime. This tool transforms 2D technical information into 3D images, allowing technicians to quickly diagnose and repair trucks using an Apple iPad, leading to reported improvements of 15-20% in service repair times. The technology provides "X-ray vision" to improve diagnostic and repair times for Peterbilt trucks.

feed.title: Ford Motor Co. beefs up tech strength with director known for global dominance
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has elected John May, CEO of Deere & Co., to its board of directors, a move analysts say underscores Ford's commitment to technology and dominance in electric and autonomous vehicles. May's experience with Deere's technologically advanced agricultural equipment and successful resolution of a recent UAW strike are seen as valuable assets for Ford's own transformation and supply chain management. The appointment expands Ford's board to 15 members, though some observers noted the company's lack of progress in increasing female board representation compared to competitors like GM.

feed.title: Apple Engineers Jumping to eVTOL
feed.summary: Several engineers are leaving Apple's self-driving car project to join the electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) industry, particularly companies like Archer Aviation and Joby Aviation. This shift is driven by the growth potential and innovation opportunities within the eVTOL sector, which is backed by significant investor capital. These engineers are bringing critical expertise in propulsion, battery systems, and radar to help eVTOL companies develop and certify air taxi services.

feed.title: Sandisk Professional G-Drive SSD review - Fast enough, secure, and durable
feed.summary: The SanDisk Professional G-Drive SSD offers portable, fast, and durable external storage for Mac users. It features USB 3.2 Type-C connectivity, considerable physical protection with IP67 rating and drop resistance, and 256-bit AES-XTS hardware encryption. While not the fastest or cheapest option, its balance of speed, ruggedness, and Mac-centric formatting makes it a professional choice for expanding MacBook storage.

feed.title: 4 Charts on Apple's $3 Trillion Market Cap
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has become the first company to achieve a $3 trillion market capitalization, briefly hitting $182.88 per share. This article highlights the immense scale of Apple's market cap through various comparisons, such as its value exceeding the total market cap of all public companies in Canada. It also discusses Apple's dominance within the U.S. market, making up nearly 22% of all U.S.-domiciled technology companies' total market capitalization.

feed.title: An industry first: Hyatt introduces room keys in Apple Wallet
feed.summary: Hyatt Hotels Corporation has launched room keys in Apple Wallet, allowing World of Hyatt guests at select locations to use their iPhone or Apple Watch to unlock guest rooms and common areas. This digital innovation aims to provide greater control and flexibility, with features like Express Mode and Power Reserve, making access near-instantaneous and secure. The technology is currently available at six hotels in the US, with plans for wider adoption.

feed.title: Polish regulator to investigate Apple's privacy policy
feed.summary: Poland's antimonopoly watchdog, UOKiK, has launched an investigation into Apple's new privacy rules for iOS devices, suspecting they might violate competition law. The regulator is examining whether Apple's policy changes, which limit third-party apps from collecting personal data for personalized ads, are a move to eliminate competitors and boost Apple's own advertising services. This investigation follows similar warnings from EU tech chief Margrethe Vestager regarding Apple's App Store practices.

feed.title: Samsung to produce STMicro's MCUs for use in iPhones
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has secured a foundry order from STMicroelectronics to produce 16-nanometer microcontroller units (MCUs) for Apple's next-generation iPhones. This marks the first time STMicroelectronics has outsourced MCU production for key clients, highlighting Samsung's growing presence in the foundry market. The deal is seen as a move by Samsung to challenge TSMC's dominance in outsourced MCU production.

feed.title: Is Saudi Aramco Stock a Good Buy? • (TADAWUL: 2222)
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Saudi Aramco stock is a good buy, considering its status as one of the world's largest companies, financial performance, and future outlook. It provides guidance on how to gain exposure to the stock through ETFs, discusses its stock history and value, and presents both bull and bear cases from analysts. The article concludes with a "hold" recommendation due to uncertainties in the global economy and the stock's relatively muted performance despite strong oil prices.

feed.title: Go With GINN as High-Margin Companies Remain Trendy
feed.summary: Investors are expected to favor quality, high-margin companies in 2022, a trend supported by the Goldman Sachs Innovate Equity ETF (GINN). GINN tracks companies with robust profitability, strong earnings growth, and effective margin management, aligning with Goldman Sachs' recommendation for clients. Several of GINN's holdings, including Alphabet, Meta Platforms, Apple, Marvell Technology, Mastercard, PayPal, Autodesk, and Etsy, are featured on Goldman's list of high-margin companies.

feed.title: BlackRock Future U.S. Themes ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:BTHM)
feed.summary: The BlackRock Future U.S. Themes ETF (BTHM) is an actively managed ETF launched on December 14, 2021, focusing on US stocks that provide exposure to emerging and structural themes. As of May 4, 2026, it trades at $40.71, reflecting a 5.6% increase since January 2026, and has assets under management of $7.89 million with a net expense ratio of 0.60%. Its top holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Scotiabank and Cineplex Transform Loyalty Landscape in Canada with the Launch of Scene+
feed.summary: Scotiabank and Cineplex have launched Scene+, an enhanced loyalty program designed to be one of Canada's largest and most flexible, offering more ways to earn and redeem points. This new program integrates SCENE and Scotia Rewards, introducing new partners like Rakuten and Scene+ Travel powered by Expedia Group, allowing members to earn and redeem points for travel, shopping, and banking, in addition to existing entertainment and dining options. Existing SCENE members and eligible Scotiabank cardholders are automatically enrolled, with their accumulated points transferred at a 1:1 value.

feed.title: What Zoom’s $85m class action lawsuit means for data privacy
feed.summary: Zoom has agreed to pay an $85 million settlement in a class action lawsuit alleging it shared user data without consent and falsely claimed end-to-end encryption. This case highlights a broader data privacy movement forcing companies to increase transparency and adjust to stricter regulations. Experts believe the lawsuit serves as a cautionary tale for the digital ecosystem and will prompt other companies to re-evaluate their data protection policies.

feed.title: Live Nation sued by Goldenvoice over alleged infringement of Coachella trademark
feed.summary: Coachella promoter Goldenvoice is suing Live Nation for alleged "contributory trademark infringement" of the Coachella name. The lawsuit targets an event called Coachella Day One 22, organized by the Twenty-Nine Palms Band of Mission Indians, whose ticketing is handled by Live Nation-owned Ticketmaster. Goldenvoice claims the event is intentionally trading on the Coachella festival's goodwill and has copied its advertising and design elements, and is seeking damages and profits from the alleged infringement.

feed.title: The Invesco QQQ Stock Price Is Up 23.7% Since January. Could There Be More Gains?
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ) has seen its stock price rise 23.7% since January, driven by major tech companies like Apple and Google, despite recent pullbacks. Investors are closely watching the Federal Reserve's monetary policy meeting for potential hawkish policies that could further impact the Nasdaq index and the QQQ ETF. While the ETF is currently in a downtrend, its present stock price may be attractive for a future rally, though a clear upward trend based on EMA crossings is yet to materialize.

feed.title: Intel to invest $7 billion in new plant in Malaysia, creating 9,000 jobs
feed.summary: Intel Chief Executive Pat Gelsinger announced a $7 billion investment to build a new chip-packaging and testing factory in Malaysia, creating over 4,000 Intel jobs and more than 5,000 construction jobs. This expansion aims to address the global semiconductor shortage, which Gelsinger expects to persist into 2023. The new advanced packaging facility is projected to begin production in 2024.

feed.title: Apple Builds New Team in Southern California to Bring More Wireless Chips In-House
feed.summary: Apple is expanding its in-house chip development by hiring engineers for a new office in Irvine, Southern California. This team will focus on creating wireless chips, potentially replacing components currently supplied by companies like Broadcom and Skyworks Solutions. The move is part of Apple's broader strategy to reduce reliance on third-party suppliers and produce more of its own components, following similar efforts with modems and other chip technologies.

feed.title: Southern Company Foundation Issues $10M in Grants to Foster HBCU Talent Through Collaboration Between INROADS, UNCF and Thurgood Marshall College Fund
feed.summary: The Southern Company Foundation has announced $10 million in grants to support a new collaboration between INROADS, UNCF, and the Thurgood Marshall College Fund. This initiative aims to foster HBCU talent by providing scholarships, endowments, and paid internships, focusing on STEM, urban planning, and architecture programs. The grants are part of Southern Company's larger $50 million multi-year commitment to enhance career readiness for students at select HBCUs.

feed.title: Sonos One review: still one of the best smart speakers, and an ideal multi-room starter
feed.summary: The Sonos One remains a top-tier smart speaker, offering excellent sound quality and versatile smart assistant integration with both Alexa and Google Assistant years after its debut. Despite minor drawbacks like less powerful microphones and no Bluetooth, its multi-room capabilities and wide streaming support make it an ideal choice for a platform-neutral audio experience. It stands out in its price range as a stellar starting point for a multi-room speaker system.

feed.title: |Product Liability & Mass Torts Digest
feed.summary: |A Texas District Court has allowed a class-action lawsuit against Apple to proceed after a plaintiff, Robert Franklin, claimed his iPhone 6 exploded and injured him. The court ruled that Franklin had standing to represent a class of iPhone 6 owners who experienced similar issues, despite Apple's arguments against "substantially similar" products and lack of a safer alternative design. While the design defect claim was dismissed with leave to amend, claims for manufacturing defect, market defect, negligence, and certain Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act claims were allowed to go forward.

feed.title: Cerner could be acquired by Oracle, says WSJ
feed.summary: Oracle is reportedly in talks to acquire health IT giant Cerner Corp. for an estimated $30 billion, which would be Oracle's largest acquisition to date. This move would significantly bolster Oracle's presence in healthcare technology, particularly in cloud computing and AI, following a trend of major tech companies investing heavily in healthcare-focused firms. The potential acquisition comes amidst a challenging year for Cerner, despite its CEO expressing optimism about long-term growth.

feed.title: Find Out Where Every ‘Spider-Man’ Movie is Streaming – Either for Free or Rental
feed.summary: This article lists where to stream every 'Spider-Man' movie, including those starring Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland. It details which subscription services offer the films for free and where they can be rented, noting that "Spider-Man: No Way Home" was only in theaters at the time of publication. The piece also explains why Spider-Man movies are not all on Disney+ with the rest of the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

feed.title: About IBS Bulgaria
feed.summary: IBS Bulgaria is helping regional banks modernize their critical systems to comply with the PSD2 directive. They developed a new open banking platform powered by IBM API Connect and IBM Cloud Pak for Integration technology, resulting in reduced integration times and significant cost reductions for their clients. This solution allows banks to quickly adapt to new regulations and services, with approximately 90% of open banking in Bulgaria now relying on IBM integration technology.

feed.title: Apple Should Make a Giant iPad as Its Smart Home Portal
feed.summary: After testing Amazon's new Echo Show 15, the author suggests Apple Inc. (AAPL) should develop a larger iPad to serve as a smart home hub. This move would enhance Apple's presence in the smart home market and diversify the utility of its iPad line. The article also touches on other Apple-related news, including COVID-19 disruptions and internal company changes.

feed.title: Amazon silenced criticism of Xi to do business in China: Report
feed.summary: A Reuters report alleges that Amazon.com Inc. quieted criticism of President Xi Jinping's book on its Chinese platform to maintain business in China. This involved scrubbing negative ratings and reviews for "The Governance of China" following requests from Beijing. This situation mirrors cases of other US companies like Apple and contrasts with others like Yahoo! and LinkedIn that have exited the Chinese market due to a challenging business environment.

feed.title: 5 things to know about Oracle after $28.3 billion acquisition of Kansas City’s Cerner
feed.summary: Oracle has announced a $28.3 billion acquisition of Cerner Corp., Kansas City's largest private employer, marking one of the year's biggest deals. This move signifies Oracle's growing push into the healthcare industry, following similar expansions by other tech giants. The acquisition brings together Oracle, an $280 billion company with 133,000 employees globally, and Cerner, valued at $23 billion with 28,000 employees worldwide, notably 13,000 in Kansas City.

feed.title: Avis Employee Used Apple Pay to Scam Customers
feed.summary: An Avis Budget employee was arrested for scamming the company out of over $5,000 by accepting customer payments via Apple Pay for car rentals and keeping the money. Alicia Leon would then modify rental agreements to show extensions, causing Avis Budget to lose $7,479 in rental fees. She faces charges of larceny and fraud.

feed.title: 15 Best Windows 10 Themes
feed.summary: This article lists the 15 best Windows 10 themes, offering various options to personalize a PC's desktop. It provides detailed instructions on how to install themes through Microsoft Store or third-party sources and highlights diverse themes ranging from 3D designs and anime to classic operating system looks and pop culture references like Star Wars and NBA. The article emphasizes that changing themes is a simple yet effective way to customize one's device and reflect personal interests.

feed.title: AT&T sells Xandr ad business to Microsoft for $1 billion
feed.summary: AT&T has sold its Xandr digital advertising business to Microsoft for an estimated $1 billion. This divestiture is part of AT&T's broader strategy to scale back its media operations, following moves with DirecTV and WarnerMedia. The sale allows Microsoft to integrate Xandr's platform into its properties, enhancing its online advertising capabilities.

feed.title: Apple’s long-term credit rating upgraded to AAA by Moody’s
authors: Jack Pitcher and Mark Gurman and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Moody’s Investors Service upgraded Apple Inc.’s long-term credit rating to Aaa, placing it in an exclusive group with Microsoft and Johnson & Johnson as the only U.S. S&P 500 corporations with the highest possible credit score. This upgrade reflects Apple's exceptional liquidity, robust earnings, and strong business profile, as the company continues to focus on a net cash-neutral position and expand into new markets like augmented reality and cars. Despite potential chip-related supply shortages impacting revenue, investors remain optimistic about Apple's long-term prospects.

feed.title: Moody's gives Apple top 'AAA' credit rating on growth promise
feed.summary: Ratings agency Moody's upgraded Apple Inc.'s long-term credit rating to 'AAA' with a stable outlook, citing its exceptional liquidity and robust earnings. This places Apple in an elite group of companies with the highest rating. While Apple's financial performance was strong in fiscal 2021, the company faces execution risks from short product cycles, shifting consumer preferences, and supply chain management.

feed.title: Market Breadth Showcases Equal-Weight Benefits
feed.summary: The article discusses the benefits of equal-weight strategies in investing, particularly highlighted by current market breadth where a few large-cap tech stocks are dominating the S&P 500. It suggests that while cap-weighted indices show strong performance due to these few stocks, equal-weight ETFs like RSP offer better diversification and can outperform when market leadership narrows, as demonstrated by its recent performance against the S&P 500.

feed.title: Apple Must Answer Shareholder Questions on Forced Labor, SEC Says
feed.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission denied Apple Inc.'s request to block a shareholder proposal seeking greater transparency on its efforts to prevent forced labor in its supply chain. This means Apple will face a vote on the proposal at its next annual shareholder meeting. The request asks for details on identified risk suppliers and actions taken against them.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Ansys and AGI continue collaboration with NORAD to track Santa's annual trip
feed.summary: Analytical Graphics Inc. (AGI), a sister company to Ansys Inc., has renewed its collaboration with NORAD's Operations Center for the annual Santa Tracker experience. This ongoing partnership leverages technological expertise to support the military unit's popular holiday program. The announcement was made as part of NORAD's yearly tradition of tracking Santa's journey.
